speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: Well met. Good morrow, Titus and Hortensius.

speaker: TITUS
text: The like to you, kind Varro.

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: Lucius!

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: What, do we meet together?

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Ay, and I think

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: One business does command us all,

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: For mine is money.

speaker: TITUS
text: So is theirs and ours.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: And, sir, Philotus’ too.

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: Good day at once.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Welcome, good brother.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: What do you think the hour?

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: Laboring for nine.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: So much?

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: Is not my lord seen yet?

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Not yet.

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: I wonder on ’t. He was wont to shine at seven.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Ay, but the days are waxed shorter with him.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: You must consider that a prodigal course

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Is like the sun’s,

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: But not, like his, recoverable. I fear

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: ’Tis deepest winter in Lord Timon’s purse:

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: That is, one may reach deep enough and yet

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Find little.

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: I am of your fear for that.

speaker: TITUS
text: I’ll show you how t’ observe a strange event.

speaker: TITUS
text: Your lord sends now for money?

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: Most true, he does.

speaker: TITUS
text: And he wears jewels now of Timon’s gift,

speaker: TITUS
text: For which I wait for money.

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: It is against my heart.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Mark how strange it shows:

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Timon in this should pay more than he owes,

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: And e’en as if your lord should wear rich jewels

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: And send for money for ’em.

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: I’m weary of this charge, the gods can witness.

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: I know my lord hath spent of Timon’s wealth,

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: And now ingratitude makes it worse than stealth.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: Yes, mine’s three thousand crowns. What’s yours?

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Five thousand mine.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: ’Tis much deep, and it should seem by th’ sum

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: Your master’s confidence was above mine,

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: Else surely his had equaled.

speaker: TITUS
text: One of Lord Timon’s men.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Flaminius? Sir, a word. Pray, is my lord

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: ready to come forth?

speaker: FLAMINIUS
text: No, indeed he is not.

speaker: TITUS
text: We attend his Lordship. Pray, signify so much.

speaker: FLAMINIUS
text: I need not tell him that. He knows you are

speaker: FLAMINIUS
text: too diligent.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Ha! Is not that his steward muffled so?

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: He goes away in a cloud. Call him, call him.

speaker: TITUS
text: Do you hear, sir?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: By your leave, sir.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What do you ask of me, my friend?

speaker: TITUS
text: We wait for certain money here, sir.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Ay,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: If money were as certain as your waiting,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: ’Twere sure enough.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Why then preferred you not your sums and bills

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: When your false masters eat of my lord’s meat?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Then they could smile and fawn upon his debts

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And take down th’ int’rest into their glutt’nous maws.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: You do yourselves but wrong to stir me up.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Let me pass quietly.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Believe ’t, my lord and I have made an end.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I have no more to reckon, he to spend.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Ay, but this answer will not serve.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: If ’twill not serve, ’tis not so base as you,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For you serve knaves.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: How? What does his cashiered

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: Worship mutter?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: No matter what. He’s poor, and

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: that’s revenge enough. Who can speak broader

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: than he that has no house to put his head in? Such

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: may rail against great buildings.

speaker: TITUS
text: O, here’s Servilius. Now we shall know some

speaker: TITUS
text: answer.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: If I might beseech you, gentlemen, to repair

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: some other hour, I should derive much from ’t. For

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: take ’t of my soul, my lord leans wondrously to discontent.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: His comfortable temper has forsook him.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: He’s much out of health and keeps his chamber.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Many do keep their chambers are not sick;

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: And if it be so far beyond his health,

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Methinks he should the sooner pay his debts

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: And make a clear way to the gods.

speaker: SERVILIUS
text: Good gods!

speaker: TITUS
text: We cannot take this for answer, sir.

speaker: FLAMINIUS
text: Servilius, help! My lord, my lord!

speaker: TIMON
text: What, are my doors opposed against my passage?

speaker: TIMON
text: Have I been ever free, and must my house

speaker: TIMON
text: Be my retentive enemy, my jail?

speaker: TIMON
text: The place which I have feasted, does it now,

speaker: TIMON
text: Like all mankind, show me an iron heart?

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Put in now, Titus.

speaker: TITUS
text: My lord, here is my bill.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Here’s mine.

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: And mine, my lord.

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: And ours, my lord.

speaker: PHILOTUS
text: All our bills.

speaker: TIMON
text: Knock me down with ’em! Cleave me to the girdle.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Alas, my lord—

speaker: TIMON
text: Cut my heart in sums!

speaker: TITUS
text: Mine, fifty talents.

speaker: TIMON
text: Tell out my blood.

speaker:
LUCIUS’
               
              MAN
text: Five thousand crowns, my lord.

speaker: TIMON
text: Five thousand drops pays that.—What yours?—And yours?

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              FIRST
               
              MAN
text: My lord—

speaker:
VARRO’S
               
              SECOND
               
              MAN
text: My lord—

speaker: TIMON
text: Tear me, take me, and the gods fall upon you!

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: Faith, I perceive our masters may throw

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: their caps at their money. These debts may well be

speaker: HORTENSIUS
text: called desperate ones, for a madman owes ’em.

speaker: TIMON
text: They have e’en put my breath from me, the slaves!

speaker: TIMON
text: Creditors? Devils!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: My dear lord—

speaker: TIMON
text: What if it should be so?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: My lord—

speaker: TIMON
text: I’ll have it so.—My steward!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Here, my lord.

speaker: TIMON
text: So fitly? Go, bid all my friends again,

speaker: TIMON
text: Lucius, Lucullus, and Sempronius, all.

speaker: TIMON
text: I’ll once more feast the rascals.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O my lord,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: You only speak from your distracted soul.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: There’s not so much left to furnish out

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: A moderate table.

speaker: TIMON
text: Be it not in thy care. Go,

speaker: TIMON
text: I charge thee, invite them all. Let in the tide

speaker: TIMON
text: Of knaves once more. My cook and I’ll provide.
